message:
refactor(terminal,core,storage): inline auto-pause logic and add TickSummary serialization support

This commit refactors the terminal auto-pause implementation and adds serialization
capabilities to TickSummary for improved persistence and replay compatibility.

## Terminal Auto-Pause Refactor (terminal/mod.rs)
- Inlined `evaluate_auto_pause()` logic directly into `refresh_snapshot()` method
- Removes separate method to avoid method resolution issues
- Maintains identical auto-pause behavior:
  - Checks `auto_pause_on_spike_hit` for combat spike detection
  - Checks `auto_pause_age_above` for max age threshold
  - Checks `auto_pause_population_below` for population minimum
- Still logs descriptive event messages and prevents duplicate pause events
- Sets `paused = true` and `speed_multiplier = 0.0` when triggered

## TickSummary Serialization (core/lib.rs)
- Added `Serialize` and `Deserialize` derives to `TickSummary` struct
- Enables persistence to DuckDB and other storage backends
- Added `#[serde(default)]` to `max_age` field for backward compatibility
- Added `#[serde(default)]` to `spike_hits` field for backward compatibility
- Ensures old persisted data without these fields deserializes with 0 values
- Critical for replay system to store/restore tick summaries

## Storage Test Fix (storage/lib.rs)
- Updated test fixture to include `max_age: 0` in TickSummary construction
- Updated test fixture to include `spike_hits: 0` in TickSummary construction
- Ensures tests compile and pass with expanded TickSummary struct
- Validates persistence round-trip with new fields

These changes improve code organization by inlining context-specific auto-pause logic,
while enabling future features that require TickSummary serialization (CSV export,
replay comparison, historical analysis).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
